title: Design an emergency stop that always wins
description: Provide a safety-stop path that halts all actuators within a bounded time regardless of software state, independent of the normal control flow.
body: Use whenever the system can command motion. Do not route the stop through the same queues or threads that might be blocked, and avoid relying on a graceful shutdown that assumes the software is healthy. Actions: implement a high-priority stop that cuts actuator power or commands a safe holding state, drive it from a watchdog that triggers on missed heartbeats or loss of control, and make the fail-safe state the default on any fault. Validation signals include a test that the stop halts all actuators within the required time even with the main loop deliberately stalled. Risk boundary: an e-stop that depends on healthy software can fail exactly when needed; treat a stop path coupled to the normal control flow as a critical safety defect and require independent validation.
